purpose: Convert 2025-2026 frontier literature signals into auditable Block 3 mechanism hypotheses and gates.
claim_boundary: Rows marked official_verified are supported by existing repo official-source notes; rows marked repo_tracked require a fresh official-page pass before paper claims.
This registry defines mechanism hypotheses and validation gates; it does not claim any mechanism has passed until a linked artifact says so.
Source is treated as event-state/regime/context evidence, not as a direct residual multiplier.

mechanisms.hypothesis: EDGAR/text should define calibration regimes and drift/coverage controls, not multiply residual scale directly.
required_gates: source-regime paired MAE/coverage audit vs core_only
coverage >= 0.88 mean and materially fewer weak cells without unacceptable width inflation
strict row-key counterfactual for source/text contribution
current_evidence: source-scaling multiplier failed paired-benefit and physical-scale gates
drift_guard diagnostic pilot improved coverage but is not yet formal temporal validation

mechanisms.hypothesis: Separate dynamic business state from static/source context, then let lane-conditioned gates decide if source should be read.
required_gates: paired MAE and coverage by target/horizon/source regime
current_evidence: existing source multiplier evidence says source must be guarded rather than always active

implementation_surface: future objectives.py or funding lane objective stack
mechanisms.hypothesis: Funding and long-horizon severity should be trained with multistep distribution alignment instead of point loss only.
required_gates: tail-slice MAE or quantile loss improvement
CQR/NC-CoPo coverage improvement or no degradation
current_evidence: funding targets are heavy-tailed and coverage weak cells concentrate in funding h14/h30
DistDF/QDF are mechanism signals, not landed local modules

mechanisms.hypothesis: The hardest temporal/source regimes should receive calibration-only or train-only weighting rather than a uniform global loss.
required_gates: paired target/horizon gains in investors h1 and funding tail cells
current_evidence: coverage weakness audit shows many cells below 0.88 despite acceptable canonical mean

implementation_surface: future source-native sparse memory lane reads
mechanisms.hypothesis: Text/EDGAR should enter as sparse event memory with recency, novelty, and coverage confidence rather than dense daily columns.
required_gates: dense source vs sparse event memory ablation
retrieved event novelty and recency telemetry
no-read fallback when source coverage is weak
current_evidence: text embedding bakeoff passed strict counterfactual gate
champion analysis found text embeddings often hurt unless gated

implementation_surface: future lightweight branch in TESF/shared trunk
mechanisms.hypothesis: A low-cost local-context branch can cover short/medium horizon regimes more efficiently than heavier universal backbones.
required_gates: paired Block 3 cell comparison against incumbent SOTA
current_evidence: full public-pack SAMformer array is partial until traffic shard completes

mechanisms.hypothesis: Specialist champions should teach a single deployable student at training time; runtime selector behavior should not be part of deployment.
required_gates: student vs specialist frontier paired gap
current_evidence: champion atlas is stable enough to define teachers
mainline value audit does not yet prove three-head superiority
